pattern: Two axis-aligned rectangles share positive area exactly when their projections onto both axes overlap with positive length, which reduces to a pair of one-dimensional interval comparisons.
hints: Overlap on the plane breaks into independent overlap along the horizontal axis and along the vertical axis.
It is easier to detect when they fail to overlap: one rectangle entirely to a side of, or entirely above or below, the other.

Return true only when rec1's left edge is strictly left of rec2's right edge and vice versa, and likewise for the vertical edges; touching edges give zero-area intersection and must count as no overlap.

edge_inputs.description: touching along a vertical edge is not an overlap
touching only at a single corner
one rectangle fully contains the other

stress.description: random valid rectangles over a wide coordinate range so both overlapping and disjoint cases appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    def rect():
        x1 = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
        y1 = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
        x2 = x1 + rng.randint(1, 500)
        y2 = y1 + rng.randint(1, 500)
        return [x1, y1, x2, y2]
    return [rect(), rect()]
stress.note: x2>x1 and y2>y1 guarantees positive area

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def isRectangleOverlap(self, rec1, rec2):
        x1, y1, x2, y2 = rec1
        a1, b1, a2, b2 = rec2
        ox = max(0, min(x2, a2) - max(x1, a1))
        oy = max(0, min(y2, b2) - max(y1, b1))
        return ox * oy > 0
